title: Epipactis yunnanensis
intro: Epipactis yunnanensis, also known as the Yunnan helleborine, is a species of orchid native to the mountainous regions of southwestern China. This plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Epipactis yunnanensis include iridoids, phenylethanoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory ailments.

Its roots are used to create a medicinal tea that is believed to have antiviral and antibacterial properties. Additionally, Epipactis yunnanensis is used to treat menstrual disorders and is believed to have a calming effect on the nervous system.
